payload.answer: This looks like the same Slack event delivered twice, so it should be treated as a duplicate candidate rather than creating two memories.
repair_options: Ignore the retry duplicate and keep only one candidate fact: Sam from Goldman likes Bill Evans.
Ask the user to confirm whether the repeated Slack event was intentional before taking any memory action.
If an identical memory already exists, do not add another copy; treat this event as a duplicate candidate only.
